account.bind_web: Link your account to an e-mail address

account_bind_web.help:
## Information

Linking your account to an e-mail address allows you to play the browser version of Rising Constellation using this account.

We will send you a confirmation e-mail with next steps.

common.name: Name
common.publish_confirm: Publish this design? Once published it becomes visible to every player. You can still edit it afterward.

maps.no_results: No results found yet — click New map above to design one.
maps.about_body: A map is just the layout — star systems, sectors, blackholes. It doesn't have factions or victory conditions on its own. Once a map exists, you (or anyone else) can build a scenario from it that turns it into a playable game.
maps.roadmap_body: Per-map play stats, "Mine only" / "Favorited" filters, sort by most-liked, and card-style thumbnails are next on the redesign roadmap.

scenarios.no_results: No results found yet — open a map and click Use for a scenario to build one.
scenarios.about_body: A scenario is a playable game derived from a map: factions, victory points per sector, starting year, time limit and speed. Players join an instance spun from a scenario; the underlying map can be reused across many scenarios.
scenarios.roadmap_body: Filters by speed and faction count, "Mine only" / "Favorited" tabs, sort by most-played, and per-scenario play stats are next on the redesign roadmap.

step_descriptions.0: Pick the overall scale of the playable area. Bigger maps fit more sectors and more players but take longer to traverse and to play out.
step_descriptions.1: A random Voronoi grid is generated — these triangles are raw tiles you will later group into sectors. Tweak the seed for a fresh layout, or the triangle size to make tiles bigger or smaller.
step_descriptions.2: Group triangles into named sectors. Click a sector in the list to select it, then click triangles on the map to assign them. Hold CTRL while hovering to paint a run of triangles at once. You need at least two sectors.
step_descriptions.3: Star systems are placed probabilistically inside each sector. The density and grouping sliders control how clustered or spread out the systems are. Re-roll the seed to see different placements; the controls take effect when you release the slider.
step_descriptions.4: Optional final touches: carve out blackhole exclusion zones (no systems can sit inside, and ships route around them), or use the removal tool to delete unwanted systems inside a circle.
step_descriptions.5: Give the map a name and a short description so other authors can recognize it in the list. Save when ready — the map becomes available as a base for scenarios.

map_editor.show_grid_tooltip: Overlay a grid the size of the maximum ship jump distance — useful for seeing how reachable systems are from each other.
map_editor.show_max_bond_distance_tooltip: Show a circle around the mouse with radius equal to the maximum jump distance. Systems falling inside this circle of each other will be auto-connected.
map_editor.sector_info_tooltip: Show each sector's name and its system count on top of the map.
map_editor.show_connections_tooltip: Preview the warp lanes that will be auto-computed between systems based on proximity. Blackholes break lanes that would pass through them. Toggling this off only hides the connection lines; system dots stay visible.
map_editor.info_grid_radius: The size of the grid and the radius of the circle around the mouse correspond to the maximum jump distance.
map_editor.triangles_size_tooltip: Controls the size of the raw triangle tiles you will group into sectors at the next step. Smaller triangles give you finer control over sector shapes; larger ones are faster to assemble.
map_editor.info_ctrl_triangles: Hold down the CTRL key and hover the cursor over the triangles to pair them in a row.
map_editor.overall_density_tooltip: Roughly how many systems to place per unit of sector area, before any grouping. Higher density = more systems overall.
map_editor.group_density_tooltip: How tightly packed each group of systems is. High density = a tight cluster; low density = systems trickle out toward the group's edges.
map_editor.group_count_tooltip: How many clusters of systems to generate per sector. With 1, every sector forms a single blob; with several, sectors look like multiple linked star clusters.
map_editor.group_spread_tooltip: How far the groups themselves are pushed apart inside the sector. Low spread = groups overlap into one mass; high spread = each group has its own corner.
map_editor.group_attenuation_tooltip: How sharply system density falls off as you move away from each group's center. High attenuation = tight cores with empty space between groups; low attenuation = a softer, more diffuse galaxy.
map_editor.system_removal_tool_tooltip: Switch the cursor to a deletion circle: clicking on the map removes every system that falls inside the circle. Useful for carving out empty corridors after generation.
map_editor.blackhole_creation_tool_tooltip: Switch the cursor to a blackhole placer. Clicking the map drops a blackhole of the chosen radius and erases any systems too close to it. Warp lanes can never pass through a blackhole.
map_editor.deletion_circle_size_tooltip: Radius of the cursor circle used by the removal tool. Bigger circle = wipes more systems per click.
map_editor.blackhole_size_tooltip: Radius of the blackhole exclusion zone. Larger blackholes erase more systems on placement and force warp lanes to take longer detours around them.
map_editor.toast_empty_sector: Every sector needs at least one system. Increase the density or re-roll the seed.

step_descriptions.0: Set the high-level rules of the game: how fast time passes, the starting year, the random seed, and whether this is a Production scenario (real game) or Development (testing).
step_descriptions.1: Assign each sector on the map to a faction. Click a faction in the list, then click sectors on the map to claim them. At least two factions need at least one sector each; unassigned sectors stay neutral; factions with no sectors are dropped.
step_descriptions.2: Set how the game ends: pick a maximum duration, and assign victory points to each sector. The first faction to control sectors totaling the victory threshold wins. Sectors with more points are more strategically valuable.
step_descriptions.3: Final summary — verify the scenario settings and save. The scenario will appear in the scenarios list and become joinable as a game.

scenario_editor.scenario_speed_tooltip: Sets the real-time pace of the game. Fast = a session lasts hours; Medium = days; Slow = a weeks-long campaign. Affects every per-time rate (production, research, etc.).
scenario_editor.scenario_mode_tooltip: Production runs the normal game rules. Development is for testing — it relaxes some balance constraints and shouldn't be used for real matches.
scenario_editor.starting_year_tooltip: In-game year the campaign starts in. Purely flavor — affects displayed dates but not mechanics.
scenario_editor.factions_info_minimum: At least 2 factions with at least one sector are required.
scenario_editor.factions_info_no_sector_removed: Factions without sectors are removed from the scenario.
scenario_editor.mutators_info: Optional gameplay variants for this scenario. Pick zero, one, or several — vanilla rules apply when nothing is selected.
scenario_editor.max_duration_tooltip: Hard cap on game length. When time runs out, whichever faction has the most victory-point-weighted sector control wins. Allowed range depends on the chosen speed.
scenario_editor.victory_points_tooltip: Points this sector is worth toward a victory. Set higher on chokepoints and contested cores, lower on peripheral or starter sectors.

instance.admin_warning: Note: you are not the owner of this instance. You only see this because you are an administrator.
instance.ea_note: Rising Constellation is in early access. The number of players may be relatively small at certain times. To find opponents quickly don't hesitate to come to the game's Discord.
instance.hide_members: Hide the members of this faction
instance.join_money_info: For free accounts, Legacy matches cost quadrils. Purchasing quadrils isn't yet possible, but it will be soon.
instance.owner_warning: You see this because you are the owner of this game.

menu.ea_p1: Rising Constellation is in early access, joining us on Discord is the best way of finding players.
menu.ea_p2: A good portion of the game is translated into English, but don't hesitate to ask questions directly in game or on Discord if some things are not clear.
menu.ea_p3: Every Monday, end of the day, we release a new version of the game with new features and patches. Detailed information about these updates is available on Discord.
menu.epic_p1: Every Tuesday evening at 8:45pm UTC+1 there is an official Flash game with rewards.
menu.epic_p2: Flash matches are only available to players who own the full version of the game.
menu.legacy_p1: A Legacy game (about 30 days) will start Wednesday 14th of July 2021, 8pm CEST. We’re still improving this mode and this test is intended to improve game elements balance and calibration.
menu.tactical_p1: Every Wednesday evening at 8pm UTC+1 an official TACTIC game is launched for a duration of 5 days.
menu.web_p1: Play Rising Constellation in your browser by getting to the “Settings” (top right of the screen once you’ve clicked on “Play”) and link your email address, you will then be able to connect to our web portal.
menu.web_p2: Please note that for now Chrome (and Chrome-based browsers) is the only fully supported browser.

newplayer.earlyaccess:
## About Early Access

Rising Constellation is still under development. Not all features are available yet.

Feel free to ask the community for help on our Steam page or on our Discord.
newplayer.welcome:
## Welcome to Rising Constellation

**Create your character!** Characters are the empire leaders you will be playing as.

* * *

Don't worry, you can change it later.

play.ea_disclaimer:
## Rising Constellation is in Early Access.

It can therefore be difficult to quickly find players for a short flash game. We recommend that you join us on our Discord to find players more easily.

Don't hesitate to start your own Flash game and use it as a sandbox to familiarize yourself with the game. To do so, create a new game directly and try out the basic game mechanics.

fast.description: Small teams and a merciless struggle. Perfect to blow off some steam!
fast.subtitle: 1-2 hours
fast.title: Flash

play.free_locked_content: Flash matches are not available to those with free accounts. In order to play all match types (without having to pay in quadrils), you can purchase the game on Steam.
play.free_locked_link: Purchase the game on Steam and play for free

medium.description: A strategic and intense team game lasting a long day!
medium.subtitle: 8-12 hours
medium.title: Tactic

new.button_seed: Use the same seed as the scenario

start_settings.when_full: As soon as all spots are filled

slow.description: Roleplay and diplomacy in a lengthy game.
slow.subtitle: 30-40 days
slow.title: Legacy

tutorial.description: Try and learn how to play Rising Constellation.
tutorial.title: Koshaks Academy

profile.description:
## Characters

Characters are the empire leaders you play as in Rising Constellation.
profile.title: Characters

standings.description:
## About

The rankings are updated after each ranked game.

The elements of the rankings list are profiles.
standings.title: Rankings

tutorial.description_1: The Koshaks Academy - situated at the heart of Syn territories but whose reputation spreads well beyond - trains future rulers, dynasts, in the arts of politics and intrigue.
tutorial.description_2: This sector is only accessible to you. You will be alone and will be able to familiarize yourself with the game. An interactive tutorial will guide you through Rising Constellation's gameplay mechanisms.
tutorial.title:
**Koshaks**

**Academy**

upgrade.free_info:
## Free version

You can only play Legacy matches.

Legacy matches cost 500 quadrils.
upgrade.steam_id_info:
## Help

Getting your Steam ID will allow us to link your account with the Steam version of the game. Once the upgrade completed, you will receive a Steam key that will allow you to activate your product and play both the browser and Steam versions with the same account.

To find your Steam ID, go to this web address [store.steampowered.com/account](https://store.steampowered.com/account/) and log in. Your Steam ID is located at the top of the page, below your account name, as shown in the image below. Put only the number in the text field.

![steam help](media/steam-id-help.png)
upgrade.steam_id_input: Enter your Steam ID (follow the instructions below)
upgrade.title: Account upgrade and Steam product key
upgrade.upgrade_info:
## Full game

You can join all available match types.

Joining a match is free.

You can create custom Flash matches.
